sourceMovie.title: Wyatt Earp
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Wyatt Earp for the same reason it broke through in 1994: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Lawrence Kasdan keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The western and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Open Range
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Wyatt Earp clicked for you, Open Range (2003) is a strong next stop: both trade in western and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Wyatt Earp?
faq.answer: Try Open Range, Young Guns, Silverado—each shares DNA with Wyatt Earp while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Wyatt Earp?
faq.answer: Wyatt Earp (1994) is best described as Western, Drama, Action, Adventure. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Wyatt Earp worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this western lane.
